items.title: James Baldwin
items.description: Complete the James Baldwin puzzle, watch the lesson clip, then answer five facts-based questions.

quiz.q: James Baldwin was best known as which of the following?
quiz.explanation: James Baldwin was a writer, essayist, novelist, and social critic.

quiz.q: Which major theme appeared often in Baldwin’s work?
quiz.explanation: Baldwin often wrote about race, identity, justice, and American life.
His work challenged injustice and pushed readers to confront hard truths.

quiz.q: Baldwin’s writing is remembered because it was often described as what?
quiz.explanation: His voice was recognized as powerful, sharp, and deeply honest.

quiz.q: Why is James Baldwin important in Black history studies?
choices: He helped shape conversations about race and equality
He invented a video game console
quiz.explanation: Baldwin helped shape conversations about race, identity, and equality.

items.title: Faith Ringgold
items.description: Complete the Faith Ringgold puzzle, watch the lesson clip, then answer five facts-based questions.

quiz.q: Faith Ringgold is widely known as which of the following?
quiz.explanation: Faith Ringgold is celebrated as an artist, writer, and storyteller.

quiz.q: Ringgold became especially famous for creating what kind of artworks?
quiz.explanation: Her story quilts combine visual art and storytelling.

quiz.q: A major purpose of Ringgold’s art was to do what?
choices: Tell stories about Black life and history
quiz.explanation: Her work often tells stories about Black life, history, family, and culture.

quiz.q: Faith Ringgold’s work is important because it blends art with what else?
quiz.explanation: Her art often blends creativity with history, identity, and social themes.

quiz.q: Why is Faith Ringgold often included in Black history and culture lessons?
choices: Her art helps preserve stories and perspectives
quiz.explanation: Her work preserves stories, perspectives, and cultural memory.
